selectedWorkflow.rationale: defrag-etcd-v1 is purpose-built for exactly this scenario: etcd fragmentation ratio exceeding 50% (here 99.97%) with all members healthy and a leader elected. It performs a rolling defragmentation one member at a time with health checks between each, prioritising followers before the leader to protect quorum — directly addressing the root cause of unreclaimed physical bbolt pages. The workflow labels match severity=critical, environment=production, and component=statefulset/etcd/database. kaResponse.analysis: All 3 etcd members have ~99.97% DB fragmentation because auto-compaction (--auto-compaction-retention=1) reclaims logical space only — 600 keys were compacted — but no defragmentation job exists to reclaim physical pages from the bbolt backend. etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes is ~64.8 MB per member while etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_use_in_bytes is only 16 KB, a 4000:1 waste ratio. No CronJob or Job exists in the namespace, etcd_disk_defrag_inflight=0 across all members, and two prior remediation attempts left the StatefulSet spec unchanged (regression detected).

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: All 3 etcd members have ~99.97% DB fragmentation because auto-compaction (--auto-compaction-retention=1) reclaims logical space only — 600 keys were compacted — but no defragmentation job exists to reclaim physical pages from the bbolt backend. etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes is ~64.8 MB per member while etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_use_in_bytes is only 16 KB, a 4000:1 waste ratio. No CronJob or Job exists in the namespace, etcd_disk_defrag_inflight=0 across all members, and two prior remediation attempts left the StatefulSet spec unchanged (regression detected).
causalChain: Signal: EtcdHighFragmentationRatio firing critical — fragmentation ratio ~99.97% on all 3 etcd members (etcd-0, etcd-1, etcd-2)
Why? etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes is ~64.8 MB but etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_use_in_bytes is only 16,384 bytes — the bbolt database backend has accumulated massive physical fragmentation

Why? Auto-compaction (--auto-compaction-retention=1) runs and reclaims logical key revisions (600 keys compacted), but compaction does NOT reclaim physical disk pages in the bbolt backend — only defragmentation does
Why? No defragmentation operation is being run: etcd_disk_defrag_inflight=0 across all members, no CronJob or Job exists in the namespace to schedule etcdctl defrag

Root cause: The etcd StatefulSet has no companion defragmentation mechanism — auto-compaction is configured but periodic defragmentation (etcdctl defrag) is absent, causing physical DB space to grow unboundedly after compaction cycles

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: The StatefulSet is the primary remediation target as it defines the etcd cluster configuration. The actual fix is deploying a companion CronJob for defrag, but since no CronJob exists, the StatefulSet (and its operational environment) is the resource requiring attention. Prior remediations targeted this StatefulSet without spec changes — the correct fix is adding a defrag CronJob to the namespace.
dueDiligence.proportionality: The remediation is well-scoped: deploy a CronJob that runs etcdctl defrag on all 3 etcd members. This is a standard etcd operational procedure. The StatefulSet itself does not need spec changes for the defrag fix — the companion CronJob is the missing piece. Listing StatefulSet as target because it is the resource that owns the etcd cluster requiring remediation.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: StatefulSet spec, all 3 pods (status, logs, events), Prometheus fragmentation metrics (current + 1-hour range), compaction metrics, defrag inflight metric, CronJob/Job resources, Prometheus alert rules, remediation history. Did not investigate: PVC storage consumption (relevant but secondary — 1Gi PVC with 64MB DB is not at capacity), network policies (not relevant to fragmentation).
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from alert → metric values → compaction-without-defrag → missing defrag job. The compaction/defrag split is a well-known etcd operational requirement. Cannot go deeper — this is the operational root cause.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by Prometheus metric queries (fragmentation ratio, db size, db in-use size, defrag inflight, compaction keys), kubectl resource inspection (no CronJob/Job found), etcd pod logs (compaction confirmed at 19:23:37), and Prometheus alert rule output (99.97% ratio, firing since 19:24:03). Remediation history from resource context confirms regression and unchanged spec. No inaccessible data sources.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: Two prior remediations (rr-395cb8377a89-47844ce0 at 19:02:47Z and rr-395cb8377a89-847a0ba5 at 18:48:44Z) both show identical pre/post spec hashes — the StatefulSet was not modified. regression_detected=true confirms these attempts failed. A new approach must add a defrag CronJob rather than modifying the StatefulSet spec.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: 1) Memory pressure causing etcd to grow — ruled out: memory usage is within limits (512Mi limit, no OOMKills, 0 restarts). 2) Write amplification from heavy workload — ruled out: only 16 KB of data is actually in use, indicating the DB is not under heavy write load. 3) One-time event (e.g., large delete) — partially supported by the 40% drop at 19:03 UTC, but the persistent 99.97% ratio across all members and the absence of any defrag job confirms this is structural, not transient. 4) etcd bug — ruled out: v3.4.27 is a stable release and the behavior matches known compaction-without-defrag fragmentation.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.95: Starting at 1.0. -0.03 for not being able to verify what the two prior remediation attempts actually did (spec hashes match but action type is unknown). -0.02 for the brief 40%/20% fragmentation dips in the range query that suggest some manual intervention occurred — the source of these dips is unverified but does not change the root cause conclusion.
